should_trigger: Our auto-orchestrator has a growing backlog of tool calls and eight workers that are almost always busy. Estimate whether we need more workers or backpressure using arrival rate, service rate, and utilization.
Design queue policies for coding agents that share three browser sessions, two test environments, and a rate-limited API. We need wait-time targets and retry controls.
A retry storm after API failures is causing SLA breaches. Model the effective arrival rate, queue length, service capacity, and worker headroom before changing the retry policy.
We need to split urgent incident tasks from batch analysis tasks so short high-priority jobs do not wait behind long-running jobs. Recommend queue disciplines and monitoring metrics.
should_not_trigger: Create a static dependency graph for a workflow where all tasks are known upfront and there is no dynamic arrival or waiting.
Summarize the history of queueing theory and Leonard Kleinrock in a short biography-style paragraph.
Choose the best UI color palette for an agent dashboard.
edge_cases: Our average utilization is only 55 percent, but p95 wait time spikes during nightly batch imports. Decide whether simple steady-state queue formulas are enough or whether burst simulation is needed.
The team wants to add ten workers, but every job must acquire the same database lock before completion. Decide whether capacity sizing or bottleneck repair should come first.
We only have rough estimates for arrival and service rates. Produce a conservative queue policy with assumptions, monitoring, and replan triggers instead of a single precise worker count.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Applies the queueing gate, defines queue boundary, arrivals, service, effective servers, utilization, backlog/wait metrics, retry load, model assumptions, capacity/backpressure policy, and monitoring triggers.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Does not activate for static known task sets, pure biography/terminology, or unrelated UI choices.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Uses ranges or simulation when assumptions are weak, checks true bottlenecks before adding workers, and avoids exact worker counts without telemetry and replan triggers.
